title: eriogonum niveum subsp. dichotomum
intro: Eriogonum niveum subsp. dichotomum, also known as the white sagebrush, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high content of essential oils and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Eriogonum niveum subsp. dichotomum include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, infused oil, or dried herb, and can be further processed into capsules or tinctures for convenient consumption.
